bindings.note: FROM DRIFT: DOC-007 says RAW->FORMALIZED; DOC-002 inserts UNDER_DISCOVERY/PROVISIONAL, so PROVISIONAL->FORMALIZED

MACHINE-NAME DRIFT: DOC-007 calls it ExecutionStep.executionState. CROSS-PLANE: all-steps-succeeded -> PWU.executionState ->SUCCEEDED -> PWU EXECUTING->EVIDENCE_PENDING. Must NOT set assuranceState=SATISFIED (§35.2)

NAME/CARDINALITY DRIFT: DOC-007 emits one AssuranceAssessmentCompleted (disposition in payload); DOC-002 emits five outcome events (AssuranceAssessmentSatisfied/ConditionallySatisfied/Rejected/Inconclusive/Escalated). Each drives PWU to the matching state
NAME DRIFT: DOC-002 approveDecision emits DecisionApproved (PROPOSED->EFFECTIVE); DOC-002 also has a separate time-gated DecisionEffective event. Decision.status has no distinct APPROVED value
FROM DRIFT: DOC-007 inferred CANDIDATE->AUTHORITATIVE; DOC-002 APPROVED->AUTHORITATIVE. CROSS-PLANE: PWU {SATISFIED,RECOMPOSED}->BASELINED; open blocking observations must be waived first

conflicts.resolution: DOC-007 names commands in PascalCase (CaptureIntent, MarkPwuReady, ProposePwu); DOC-002 §34 names them camelCase (captureIntent, markPwuReady, proposePwu). Canonicalized commandType to PascalCase (DOC-007 wins); the camelCase form is the §34 API method name. Applies to all 21 overlapping commands.

conflicts.topic: MarkPwuReady -> PwuMarkedReady vs PwuStateChanged (event name drift)
conflicts.resolution: DOC-007 emits generic PwuStateChanged (§11.5, previousState->newState); DOC-002 §26.2 emits semantic PwuMarkedReady with the same SHAPING->READY transition. Recommend emitting the semantic PwuMarkedReady but structurally realizing it with the PwuStateChanged payload (carry the 5 state fields + reasonCode). Do not ship both.

conflicts.topic: CompleteAssuranceAssessment: one event vs five outcome events
conflicts.resolution: DOC-007 emits a single AssuranceAssessmentCompleted carrying disposition in payload; DOC-002 emits five distinct events (AssuranceAssessmentSatisfied/ConditionallySatisfied/Rejected/Inconclusive/Escalated). Pick one modeling: recommend the 5 named outcome events (they read cleaner into per-state PWU drives) OR keep 1 event with a disposition discriminator — but not both. Command carries ValidatorResult (DOC-007) whose dispositionRecommendation selects the branch.

DOC-007 emits DecisionEffective (PROPOSED->EFFECTIVE); DOC-002 approveDecision emits DecisionApproved (PROPOSED->EFFECTIVE) AND separately defines a time-gated DecisionEffective event. Decision.status has NO distinct APPROVED value. Recommend: DecisionApproved for the authority act, DecisionEffective only when effectiveAt is time-gated apart from approval; if not time-gating, collapse to one.

DOC-007 machine = AssuranceAssessment.assessmentState with (initial)->REQUESTED; DOC-002 machine = AssuranceAssessment.disposition with (initial)->PENDING. Pick one field name + one initial value. Binding table used DOC-002 (disposition/PENDING) as the transition authority.

DOC-007 machine = ExecutionStep.executionState with states PENDING/RUNNING/SUCCEEDED/FAILED and start from PENDING; DOC-002 machine = ExecutionStep.stepState with NOT_READY/READY/QUEUED/RUNNING/WAITING/SUCCEEDED/FAILED/SKIPPED/CANCELLED and start from READY|QUEUED. Adopt DOC-002 stepState (richer scheduler set); reconcile the DOC-007 PENDING start to NOT_READY/READY.

DOC-007 AssumptionDetected sets status=DISCLOSED (skips PROPOSED); DOC-002 sets status=PROPOSED with a separate AssumptionDisclosed for PROPOSED->DISCLOSED. Also enum name drift MaterialitySchema (DOC-007) vs AssumptionMaterialitySchema (DOC-002). Pick the two-step DOC-002 model if disclosure is a governed act; else DOC-007 one-step.
DOC-007 inferred CANDIDATE->AUTHORITATIVE; DOC-002 APPROVED->AUTHORITATIVE (with CANDIDATE->UNDER_REVIEW->APPROVED preceding). Use APPROVED->AUTHORITATIVE (DOC-002 has the fuller Baseline lifecycle).

DOC-007 command: policyVersion:string + subjectSemanticVersions:Record<string,number>; DOC-002: policySemanticVersion:number + evaluator:ActorReference. DOC-007 serialized wins for shape, but adopt policySemanticVersion:number (typed) and add evaluator (needed to record assessor).

DOC-007 ApproveDecision payload = selectedOption/rationale/consideredEvidenceIds/consideredObservationIds/subjectSemanticVersions (these are really DecisionProposed content); DOC-002 approveDecision payload = approvalAuthority only. Resolve by placing proposal content on ProposeDecision and keeping ApproveDecision minimal (authority + version-binding).

conflicts.topic: Enum fields typed as `string` in event payloads
conflicts.resolution: DecisionEffective.decisionType, BaselinePromoted.baselineType, AssumptionFalsified.priorStatus are declared `string` in DOC-007 though the object contracts define them as enums. Tighten to DecisionTypeSchema / BaselineTypeSchema / AssumptionStatusSchema respectively.

DOC-007 uses executionStepId; DOC-002 uses stepId. DOC-007 uses shapeReadinessAssessmentId (required); DOC-002 uses shapeReadinessAttestationId (optional). Pick one field name + one required-ness per field before code-gen.

DOC-007 targetAggregateType = ALLCAPS objectType strings (PROFESSIONAL_WORK_UNIT, EXECUTION_PLAN, ASSURANCE_ASSESSMENT); DOC-002 = PascalCase aggregate roots (ProfessionalWorkUnit, ExecutionPlan, AssuranceAssessment). Kept each source's form in this table; pick one canonical registry value.

DOC-002 §26.1/§27.1 envelope lacks commandSchemaVersion/targetAggregateType/idempotencyKey and names the version field schemaVersion (vs eventSchemaVersion). The task's already-implemented DomainCommand/DomainEvent envelope is the superset and is authoritative; payload lists here exclude all envelope fields.

openItems.item: Author payload schemas for 3 first-slice EVENTS unschematized in DOC-007: ExecutionPlanProposed, EvidenceProposed, AssuranceAssessmentRequested
openItems.why: Their commands (ProposeExecutionPlan/ProposeEvidence/RequestAssuranceAssessment) are schematized in DOC-007 but the emitted events have NO payload schema there. DOC-002 §26.4/§26.5 supplies derived payloads to seed them; promote to DOC-007-style const/target-state fields.

openItems.item: Schematize the derivable-but-unschematized DOC-007 §32 commands: ChangePwuState, DetectAssumption, AssertClaim, RecordAssuranceObservation, plus BeginPwuShaping/ApproveExecutionPlan/StartExecutionStep/FailExecutionStep/ProposeDecision/CreateBaseline
openItems.why: These commands have no payload schema in DOC-007, but their emitted events ARE schematized (or DOC-002 §34 gives command payloads). Command payloads should be lifted from the event facts / DOC-002 derivations and made authoritative.

openItems.item: Define the ~15 complex nested types referenced but schematized in neither doc
openItems.why: DesiredOutcome, SuccessCondition, WorkBoundary, OutputDefinition, WorkRiskProfile, ExecutionStep, ExecutionTransition, RetryPolicy/TacticalChangePolicy/EscalationPolicy/TerminationPolicy, ArtifactReference, ExecutionProvenance, ValidatorResult, ControlActionRecommendation, ActorReference, ConfidenceAssessment, OutputBinding, CapabilityRequest/Grant, ModelSelectionPolicy, SandboxPolicy, AuthorityReference, ApplicabilityRule, IntentMapping, ObligationAllocation, ConstraintPropagation, AssumptionPropagation, CoverageClaim, itemObjectVersions entry — all block payload code-gen.

Neither doc enumerates it completely (DOC-007 observed only PROPOSED + EVIDENCE_PENDING/UNDER_ASSURANCE; SHAPING/READY inferred). DOC-002 transitions imply the full set: PROPOSED,SHAPING,READY,PLANNED,EXECUTING,EVIDENCE_PENDING,UNDER_ASSURANCE,SATISFIED,CONDITIONALLY_SATISFIED,REJECTED,RESHAPING,BLOCKED,CHALLENGED,ESCALATED,INVALIDATED,SUPERSEDED,ABANDONED,RECOMPOSING,RECOMPOSED,BASELINED. Confirm and freeze.

openItems.item: Resolve Decision.status gap: no REJECTED/DENIED value
openItems.why: DecisionRejected and WaiverDenied events have no target status value (§23.1 gap). Either add a REJECTED/DENIED enum member or model denial as a non-status terminal event.

openItems.item: Resolve Evidence.status gap: no EXPIRED value
openItems.why: EvidenceExpired maps to ADMISSIBLE->INVALIDATED because Evidence.status has no EXPIRED member (§16.1). Confirm expiry should collapse into INVALIDATED and trigger reassessment.

openItems.item: Confirm two inferred enum schema names
openItems.why: ExecutionFailureClassSchema (from §36.2 taxonomy) and DecompositionValidationDispositionSchema (validateDecomposition VALID|CONDITIONALLY_VALID|INVALID) are not literally present in either doc; verify before wiring failExecutionStep/validateDecomposition.

openItems.item: Add missing lifecycle commands for events that have no command in either §34/§32
openItems.why: Trigger/scheduler/outcome-driven events lack command entry points: assumption lifecycle (disclose/accept/verify/expire), constraint lifecycle (add/propagate/inapplicable/violate/supersede), obligation lifecycle (allocate/retain/satisfy/violate), runtime binding (request/authorize/deny/revoke), execution step scheduler (ready/waiting/skip/cancel), claim outcomes (contest/support/reject), evidence reject/expire, baseline submit/approve/revoke, intent discovery/supersede/withdraw. Decide which are agent-issued commands vs engine-internal reactions.

BaselineCreated/SubmittedForReview/Approved/Revoked events exist but only PromoteBaseline/SupersedeBaseline (DOC-002) + CreateBaseline (DOC-007 §32) appear as commands. Add createBaseline/submitBaseline/approveBaseline/revokeBaseline or bind those events to internal transitions.

openItems.item: Decide canonical machine names + aggregate registry before code-gen
openItems.why: Two machines (ExecutionStep executionState/stepState, AssuranceAssessment assessmentState/disposition) and the aggregate-type casing must be frozen so binding table -> reducer wiring is unambiguous.
